name: sulis-execution
description: Work Package Executor — three-layer architecture (per v0.9.0) plus plan-mode-style Step 1.5 plan generation (v0.10.0+). v0.10.6 fixes a false-positive in _detect_branch_ci: the v0.10.4 substring-grep heuristic matched any Conventional Commits prefix anywhere in a workflow YAML — including paths-ignore filters. A real deploy-on-push-to-dev workflow with `paths-ignore: ['docs/**']` was misclassified as branch CI (because `docs/` matched the substring), causing wpx-pipeline to poll for 45 min waiting for check-runs that never appeared. Replaced with a structural parser that walks each .github/workflows/*.y[a]ml file looking specifically for `branches:` declarations (under on.push or on.pull_request), parsing inline lists and block lists. Explicitly rejects `branches-ignore`, `paths`, `paths-ignore`, `tags`, and comments. Two new regression tests pinned: paths-ignore must not register; branches-ignore must not register. v0.10.5 shipped the pytest test suite + two bug fixes (already-merged branch detection + multi-table INDEX parsing). v0.10.4 fixed emit_ok exit_code + no-branch-CI hang. v0.10.3 closed the auto-draft INDEX-registration gap. v0.10.2 fixed wpx-step12 dispatcher correctness. v0.10.1 fixed wpx-* tool discoverability. 43 tests across unit/ + integration/. Composes with CP-01..CP-05, AAF-01..AAF-09, EP-02/03/07, RGB-01..03. v0.10.7: wpx-pipeline's Step 10a health check now auto-detects the health path from --smoke-cmd's URL (e.g. `curl -sf https://staging/health` → /health), with --health-path flag for explicit override. Fixes the bug where APIs whose root returns 404 by design (health only at /health) produced spurious 10-minute hangs followed by health_status: unhealthy BLOCKERs. Backward-compatible: falls back to / when no URL is in the smoke command. Three regression tests pinned (49 tests total). v0.11.0: implements ADR-212 release-train / merge-queue (wpx-train tool). New per-batch path replaces the per-WP wpx-pipeline default; wpx-pipeline remains for hotfix / solo-ship via /sulis-execution:run-wp WP-X --force-single. wpx-train discovers eligibility from INDEX.md + origin branch list + per-WP CI status (no separate queue file per ADR-212 D6 amended); packs into batches up to 5 WPs; rebases each branch onto the previous rebased branch's HEAD; runs bundled-tip CI; sequential squash-merges; one deploy + health + smoke per batch. Failure modes: per-WP rebase conflict flips INDEX status to step-7-blocked + writes BLOCKER + train continues with the rest; bundled-tip CI fail flips all in-batch to step-7-blocked + writes train BLOCKER with file-overlap-heuristic culprit; post-merge deploy/health/smoke fail triggers full revert sequence (single wrapper commit on dev) + branch restoration with force-push guard (verifies origin/branch SHA equals rebased_to_sha before resetting to pre_train_sha). Step 11 security review stays per-WP, parallel, after batch deploy. New status enum values: step-7-complete (executor finished, queued for train), step-7-held (founder-paused), step-7-blocked (train failed for this WP). Polling + git/gh helpers extracted from wpx-pipeline into _wpxlib.py (Phase 0 refactor; no behaviour change to existing wpx-pipeline path). 101 tests pass (49 original + 52 new). Skill changes: /sulis-execution:run-wp default flips INDEX status (no auto-dispatch); /sulis-execution:run-all calls wpx-train run at batch boundaries; /sulis-execution:status surfaces eligible-count + trigger state; lifecycle.md documents the per-batch/per-WP split. Deferred to v0.12.0: primitive-driven batch_hint (cross-plugin SEA change); O(log N) bisect fallback to the file-overlap heuristic. v0.12.0: implements the Change Work Standard (CW-01..CW-08 published by SRD v1.22.0) — every piece of work is now bounded by a change branch + worktree, with the executor + train operating inside it. New sulis-change CLI tool with five subcommands: start (provision branch + worktree), adopt (retrofit in-flight work via stash + cherry-pick), finish (rebase + merge/PR + cleanup), list (enumerate active changes), status (one change's summary). Two-level worktree hierarchy — change worktree at the top, executor WP worktrees nested inside, branching off the change branch (not dev). wpx-pipeline and wpx-train gain a --base-branch parameter (default: dev) for backward compatibility; the calling session passes the change branch name when inside a change worktree. Skill markdown updated: run-wp Step 0 detects change context; run-all wpx-train invocation passes --base-branch; status surfaces the active change. Markdown-only update to sulis-concierge — the concierge picks a primitive + slug from founder intent, runs sulis-change start, then invokes downstream specialists inside the change worktree. References + READMEs document the two-level worktree hierarchy and the change-bounded lifecycle. 155 tests pass (101 prior + 54 new for sulis-change). Backward-compatible: when invoked outside a change worktree, --base-branch defaults to dev and behaviour is identical to v0.11.0. v0.13.0: ships the sulis-execution SDK at plugins/sulis-execution/sdk/ — a typed Python + TypeScript + MCP-server surface for the 10 CLI tools (38 operations). Schema source: sulis-execution.openapi.yaml (OpenAPI 3.1). Transport: Subprocess + JSON-on-stdout per agent-consumable-sdk-spec v0.2.0 Part 4.3 (primary, for the Python + TS clients); JSON-RPC over stdio / MCP per Part 4.2 (secondary, for the MCP server consumed by Claude Desktop / Cursor). Error model: outcome-category hierarchy (ProtocolError / ExpectedError / InternalError) with wpx-domain extensions; load-bearing rule that `outcome: blocker` is a normal result not an exception. Python package `sulis-execution` (Pydantic v2; sync + async clients); TypeScript package `@sulis-ai/execution` (typed interfaces; async-only). MCP server `sulis-execution-mcp` reads the OpenAPI spec at startup and registers each operation as an MCP tool with the operation's description as the LLM-facing prompt. Documentation per Diátaxis quadrants (~26 doc files): tutorials per language + per transport; how-to guides for error handling, configuration, mock-for-testing; reference catalogues for operations, errors, transport bindings, configuration, MCP tools; explanation essays for mental model, error categories, blocker-not-exception, retry strategy, field case conventions, versioning policy; recipes (run-train-after-executors, ship-change-end-to-end). Rubric validation pass produces PASS-WITH-RATIONALE verdict (0 MUST failures; 6 SHOULD-failures deferred with rationale; 99 PASS; 17 N/A; 2 UNVERIFIABLE — see sdk/SDK-VALIDATION-REPORT.md). Total test count across the plugin: 206 tests (155 CLI + 25 Python SDK + 17 MCP + 9 TypeScript). All existing tests pass; zero regressions. The underlying CLI tools (wpx-pipeline, wpx-train, sulis-change, etc.) are unchanged — the SDK wraps them as the public surface. v0.14.0: SDK v0.2.0 naming sweep — every method name is now self-describing without insider knowledge. Resource renames: step12 → lifecycle, wp → work_package. Method renames (9): step12.wrap → lifecycle.complete, wp.read_frontmatter → work_package.read_metadata, index.add_wp → index.add, index.propagate_blocked → index.mark_downstream_blocked, index.sync_auto_drafts → index.register_pending_drafts, findings.auto_draft_wp → findings.draft_remediation, journal.seed_plan → journal.create_plan, journal.mark_plan_item → journal.update_plan_item, journal.record_postdeploy → journal.record_security_verdict. Result type + MCP tool names renamed in parallel. The OpenAPI spec gains an x-cli-subcommand extension on the 9 renamed operations so the SDK method dispatches to the correct underlying CLI subcommand (e.g. lifecycle.complete → wpx-step12 wrap). Migration guide at sdk/docs/migrations/v0.1-to-v0.2.md with per-rename before/after snippets + sed expressions. Underlying CLI binaries + subcommands unchanged. 209 tests pass (155 wpx + 25 Python SDK + 9 TS + 20 MCP). Triggered by peer-SDK test session feedback that surfaced naming-coherence as a real first-time-user obstacle; rubric v0.2.0 added 5 checks (4.01a, 5.01a, 10.A.04a, 12.09, 12.10) catching this class. v0.15.0: ships .mcp.json at the plugin root so the sulis-execution-mcp MCP server auto-loads whenever the plugin is enabled. Any Claude session that loads sulis-execution gets the 38 typed tools natively (addressable as `mcp__sulis-execution-mcp__<tool_name>`); no manual claude_desktop_config.json editing required. Per Claude Code's plugin manifest support for mcpServers, servers start when the plugin is enabled and connect at session startup; /reload-plugins reconnects them mid-session. Env vars (SULIS_EXECUTION_REPO_ROOT, SULIS_EXECUTION_PROJECT, WPX_DIR) have sensible defaults derived from CLAUDE_PLUGIN_ROOT; can be overridden per-shell. Prerequisite: the sulis-execution-mcp Python package must be installed (pip install plugins/sulis-execution/sdk/mcp-server/) — the .mcp.json references it as a console script. v0.15.1: three real bug fixes surfaced by a live executor session against the platform repo. (1) wpx-index flip-status argparse `choices=` list was missing step-7-complete / step-7-held / step-7-blocked — added in v0.11.0 to the SDK + train eligibility logic but never propagated to the CLI's argparse. SDK declared these statuses valid, train evaluated on them, but the CLI rejected them. Three new regression tests pin the contract. (2) wpx-train's rebase_branch_in_clone + restore_branch_to_pre_train used `git fetch origin <branch>` against a clone created by `gh repo clone --depth 100` (single-branch shallow). Under single-branch mode, that fetch updates FETCH_HEAD but does NOT create refs/remotes/origin/<branch> — the subsequent `git checkout -B <branch> origin/<branch>` failed. Fixed by using explicit refspec form: `git fetch origin <branch>:refs/remotes/origin/<branch>`. (3) The same path's `git push --force-with-lease` interacted badly with the refspec-form fetch's freshly-created tracking ref, producing `stale info` errors. Fixed by capturing pre-rebase SHA from `git rev-parse origin/<branch>` and using explicit `--force-with-lease=<branch>:<pre_rebase_sha>` form — same safety guarantee, deterministic comparison. wpx-pipeline path (worktree-based, not clone-based) is unaffected. 158 tests pass. v0.15.2: two more bugs from the same live executor session. (1) DEPLOY_DEFAULT_CAP was hard-coded to 30 min and not exposed as a CLI flag. A real platform-repo deploy took ~35 min, hit the cap, triggered ADR-212 full revert even though the deploy actually succeeded. Fix: raised default to 60 min + added `--deploy-cap` flag to both wpx-train and wpx-pipeline (seconds; overrides default). (2) restore_branch_with_guard failed to restore branches when GitHub's delete-branch-on-merge had auto-deleted them. The function did `git fetch origin <branch>` first; fetch returned non-zero because origin had no such ref; restore was skipped → user's feature branches stayed deleted from origin. Fix: probe `git ls-remote --heads origin <branch>` first. If branch is missing on origin (auto-deleted), push pre_train_sha as a fresh branch create (no force needed — nothing to clobber). If present, existing guard logic applies. Two new regression tests cover both auto-deleted-branch sub-paths (success + push-fails). 160 wpx tests pass.
